commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=5e3a3529f897755682123966062973e4a2b7704a)
commands.review_summary:
The PR replaces raw `.slice(0, N)` truncation in three Browser plugin snapshot paths with `Array.from(...).slice(...).join("")` and adds a colocated truncation test file.

PR surface: Source +4, Tests +47. Total +51 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main uses raw `.slice(0, 100)` and `.slice(0, 120)` on browser snapshot labels, so an astral character at the cut boundary can leave a dangling surrogate half. I did not run a live browser snapshot reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The contributor needs to update the patch and provide after-fix real behavior proof; ClawSweeper should not repair-marker this external PR while that proof gate remains contributor-owned.

target.title: fix(browser): truncate element text on code-point boundaries to avoid lone surrogates
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Three browser snapshot/text-extraction paths truncate element text content with
raw `.slice(0, N)`. When an emoji or other astral character straddles the cut
point, the raw slice keeps only the high-surrogate half, producing a lone
`\uD83D` in the LLM context — malformed UTF-16.

Affected paths:
- `cdp.ts`: `findCursorInteractiveElements` evaluates page script that truncates
  `textContent` at 100 code units
- `pw-tools-core.snapshot.ts`: `collectSnapshotUrls` truncates anchor label at
  120 code units
- `routes/agent.snapshot.ts`: `collectChromeMcpSnapshotUrls` truncates anchor
  label at 120 code units

## Why This Change Was Made

Since the affected code runs in the browser page context (via CDP `Runtime.evaluate`
and Playwright `page.evaluate`), the SDK's `truncateUtf16Safe` is not importable.
Instead, `Array.from(str).slice(0, N).join("")` splits the string by Unicode code
points — ensuring surrogate pairs are never split.

## User Impact

No user-facing behavior change. Element text sent to the LLM context now contains
valid UTF-16 regardless of where emoji fall in the truncated string.

## Evidence

- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `pnpm test extensions/browser` — 59 tests passed (6 new + 53 existing)

### Real behavior proof

```text
$ node --import tsx proof-browser-text-boundary.mts

=== ASCII within limit ===
  input length (code units): 11
  input length (code points): 11
  result: hello world
  hasDanglingSurrogate: false
  PASS

=== Long ASCII truncated at boundary ===
  input length (code units): 120
  input length (code points): 120
  result: xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx…
  hasDanglingSurrogate: false
  PASS

=== Emoji at boundary — dropped whole, not split ===
  input length (code units): 106
  input length (code points): 105
  result: xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx…
  hasDanglingSurrogate: false
  PASS

=== Multiple emoji — one kept, one dropped ===
  input length (code units): 107
  input length (code points): 105
  result: xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx…
  hasDanglingSurrogate: false
  PASS

=== No dangling surrogates in output ===
  input length (code units): 102
  input length (code points): 101
  result: xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx…
  hasDanglingSurrogate: false
  PASS

==================================================
PASS: 5/5 tests passed
No dangling surrogates in any output: ✅
```

Each test exercises the exact `Array.from(str).slice(0, N).join("")` pattern
used in the three changed paths, showing that emoji at the boundary are
dropped entirely rather than split into lone surrogates.

## Tests

`extensions/browser/src/browser/browser-text-truncation.test.ts` — 6 new tests
covering the truncation pattern: ASCII pass-through, long ASCII truncation,
emoji-at-boundary (lone-surrogate assertion), multiple emoji, empty string,
and emoji-before-boundary pass-through.
